cot_prefix: Okay, let me read the passage carefully and figure out the answer to the question. The question is: "When he was stopped by a policeman, he ___." The possible answers are: (A) was driving at a high speed (B) was driving onto the main road (C) was going to stop his car (D) was driving slowly Let me analyze the events in the passage step by step. The passage says that Mr. Williams saw a sign that said "SLOW" at a crossroad. According to the rules mentioned in the beginning of the passage, at crossroads with a "SLOW" sign, drivers should slow down but do not have to stop unless there is something coming along the main road. The passage states: "Mr. Williams ... came to a crossroad. It had a 'SLOW' sign. He slowed down when he came to the main road, looked both ways to see that nothing was coming, and then drove across without stopping completely." So, after slowing down, Mr. Williams looked both ways and then drove across the crossroad without fully stopping. Then the police officer stopped him and said: "You didn't stop at that crossing." Mr. Williams responded by saying that the sign said "SLOW" and that he did go slow. The officer later realized he was in the wrong street after checking the surroundings. So, when the policeman stopped Mr. Williams, what was he doing? Let me look at each option: (A) was driving at a high speed – No, the passage says he had slowed down. It is clear that Mr. Williams was driving slowly at the time of crossing the crossroad. (B) was driving onto the main road – The passage says he was on a crossroad where the sign said "SLOW". According to the rules mentioned, at such crossroads, drivers slow down but do not have to stop unless something is coming along the main road. Since Mr. Williams was driving across the crossroad (presumably from a minor road onto the main road), it seems that he was driving onto the main road. However, the passage doesn't explicitly say whether he was on the main road or not at the moment he was stopped by the policeman. (C) was going to stop his car – No, the passage says he "drove across without stopping completely." (D) was driving slowly – Yes, the passage says he "slowed down when he came to the main road" and then "drove across without stopping completely." This indicates that
